generic: This repo has the commit hygiene of a raccoon in a dumpster.
I've seen grocery lists with better version control.
Your branch names read like cries for help.
Every commit here is a tiny act of optimism that did not pay off.
This codebase isn't legacy, it's a crime scene with good test coverage.
The git log reads like a diary nobody should have published.
You don't write code, you negotiate with it and lose.
This repo is held together by hope and one very tired developer.
Somewhere a computer science professor felt a chill and didn't know why.
wip_commits: {n} commits literally say 'wip'. Inspiring transparency, terrible planning.
{n} 'wip' commits. We get it, everything is a work in progress, including you.
You have {n} 'wip' commits. The 'w' stands for 'why'.
fix_commits: {n} commits are some variation of 'fix'. Fixing what? The previous fix.
{n} commits about fixes. The bugs are clearly winning the long game.
final_commits: {n} commits contain the word 'final'. None of them were.
You named {n} commits 'final'. The lie detector determined that was a lie.
typo_commits: {n} commits just fix typos. Slow down, Shakespeare.
{n} typo-fix commits. Spellcheck is right there, in the same building.
revert_commits: {n} reverts. Two steps forward, {n} steps confidently backward.
{n} revert commits. Undo is not a development strategy, but okay.
lowercase: Not a single capital letter in your commit messages. Even your commits are tired.
Your commit messages whisper in lowercase. Speak up, the future maintainer can't hear you.
short_messages: Average commit message length: {n} characters. That's not a message, that's a grunt.
Your commit messages average {n} characters. Twitter would reject them for lacking substance.
long_messages: Average commit message: {n} characters. We're writing essays now, are we? Nobody read it.
Your commits average {n} characters. This is a commit log, not your memoir.
night_owl: {n}% of your commits happen after midnight. Sleep is also a dependency.
{n}% of commits made past midnight. The bugs you wrote at 3 AM say hello.
single_author: One author. You're the only one here. Talking to yourself in commit form.
Solo project. Every bug has exactly one suspect, and it's you.
compliments: ...but honestly, the code itself isn't bad. Don't let it go to your head.
...still, you shipped something. That's more than most. Barely.
...all that said, it runs. Against all odds, it runs.
